title: Colombia Votes in High-Stakes Presidential Election
subtitle: Voters choose between continuing peace talks or taking a tougher approach to rising violence.

summary.en: Colombia is holding a presidential election that will decide the country's future path. Voters are choosing between candidates who support current peace efforts and those who want a tougher approach to crime. The election happens ten years after a historic peace deal was signed with rebel groups. Violence has returned to many parts of the country, making security a top issue for citizens. The results will determine if the government continues to negotiate with armed groups or starts a more aggressive crackdown.
summary.zh: 哥倫比亞正舉行總統大選，這將決定該國未來的走向。選民將在支持現行和平努力的候選人，以及主張對犯罪採取更強硬手段的候選人之間做出選擇。此次選舉距離與叛亂團體簽署歷史性和平協議已過十年。由於暴力事件在國內許多地區重現，安全問題已成為公民最關切的議題。選舉結果將決定政府是繼續與武裝團體談判，還是採取更激進的掃蕩行動。

paragraphs.en: Citizens in Colombia are heading to the polls to vote in a presidential election that could change the future of the country. This election is very important because it asks voters to decide how to handle the long history of fighting between the government and armed rebel groups. Ten years ago, Colombia signed a famous peace deal with the Farc, a large rebel group. While that agreement gave many people hope for a peaceful future, violence has recently returned to many areas. The election is seen as a test of the policies of current President Gustavo Petro.
paragraphs.zh: 哥倫比亞公民正前往投票所，參與一場可能改變國家未來的總統大選。這場選舉至關重要，因為選民必須決定如何處理政府與武裝叛亂團體之間長期的衝突歷史。十年前，哥倫比亞與大型叛亂團體「哥倫比亞革命武裝力量」（Farc）簽署了一項著名的和平協議。儘管該協議曾讓許多人對和平的未來抱持希望，但近期暴力事件已在許多地區死灰復燃。這場選舉被視為對現任總統裴卓（Gustavo Petro）政策的一場考驗。

paragraphs.en: According to The Guardian, there are 14 candidates on the ballot, but the race is mainly between three people. One of the leading candidates is Iván Cepeda, who is an ally of President Petro. Cepeda wants to continue the "total peace" plan. This plan involves talking to the remaining rebel groups to sign agreements and stop the fighting. Supporters of this plan often point to other positive changes made by the current government, such as raising the minimum wage. However, critics argue that the plan has not worked well because criminal groups have used the ceasefire — an agreement to stop fighting for a period of time — to reorganize and continue their activities.
paragraphs.zh: 根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，選票上有十四位候選人，但選情主要集中在三人之間。其中一位領先的候選人是裴卓總統的盟友塞佩達（Iván Cepeda）。塞佩達希望延續「全面和平」計畫，該計畫旨在與剩餘的叛亂團體進行對話，以簽署協議並停止戰鬥。此計畫的支持者常指出現任政府的其他正面變革，例如提高最低工資。然而，批評者認為該計畫成效不彰，因為犯罪集團利用停火協議——即暫時停止戰鬥的約定——來重整旗鼓並繼續其非法活動。

paragraphs.en: Running against Cepeda are two candidates, Abelardo de la Espriella and Paloma Valencia, who promise to use a much stronger hand against armed groups. De la Espriella is a lawyer who wants to copy the methods used in El Salvador to fight gangs. While those methods did lower violence in El Salvador, they also led to many complaints about human rights abuses. Paloma Valencia is a follower of former president Álvaro Uribe. During his time in office, Uribe was known for using military force to fight rebels, which led to a decrease in their power but also caused harm to many civilians.
paragraphs.zh: 與塞佩達競爭的另外兩位候選人是德拉艾斯皮耶拉（Abelardo de la Espriella）與瓦倫西亞（Paloma Valencia），他們承諾對武裝團體採取更強硬的手段。身為律師的德拉艾斯皮耶拉希望效法薩爾瓦多打擊幫派的方法。雖然這些手段確實降低了薩爾瓦多的暴力事件，但也引發了許多關於侵犯人權的投訴。瓦倫西亞則是前總統烏里韋（Álvaro Uribe）的支持者。在烏里韋執政期間，他以動用軍事力量打擊叛軍聞名，這雖然削弱了叛軍勢力，但也對許多平民造成了傷害。

paragraphs.en: Both de la Espriella and Valencia have spoken about their support for Donald Trump. They believe that Colombia should take a more aggressive approach to security, similar to the pressure that Trump has put on other countries in the region to stop criminal groups. This difference in opinion has created a deep divide among the people of Colombia. Some voters feel that the government has been too soft on crime, while others worry that a return to military-focused policies will lead to more suffering for ordinary people.
paragraphs.zh: 德拉艾斯皮耶拉與瓦倫西亞都曾表達對川普（Donald Trump）的支持。他們認為哥倫比亞應採取更積極的安全方針，類似於川普對該地區其他國家施壓以遏止犯罪集團的做法。這種意見分歧在哥倫比亞民眾之間造成了深刻的裂痕。部分選民認為政府對犯罪過於軟弱，而另一部分人則擔心回歸軍事導向的政策會導致平民遭受更多苦難。

paragraphs.en: In the capital city of Bogotá, voters have different views on what should happen next. Maria Eugenia, a 57-year-old seamstress, told The Guardian that she wants the government to take a very strong stand against criminal groups. She said that even though there might be a high human cost, she believes it is necessary to clean up the country. She is voting for de la Espriella because she feels that the violence in rural areas has become impossible to control. On the other hand, younger voters like 26-year-old Cristian Morales have different concerns. Many people are worried that returning to old ways of fighting will not solve the root causes of the conflict.
paragraphs.zh: 在首都波哥大，選民對於未來該何去何從有著不同的看法。五十七歲的裁縫師瑪麗亞·尤金妮亞（Maria Eugenia）告訴《衛報》，她希望政府對犯罪集團採取極其強硬的立場。她表示，儘管可能付出高昂的人道代價，但她認為這是整頓國家所必需的。她將票投給德拉艾斯皮耶拉，因為她覺得農村地區的暴力已變得無法控制。另一方面，像二十六歲的克里斯蒂安·莫拉萊斯（Cristian Morales）這樣的年輕選民則有不同的擔憂。許多人擔心，重回舊有的戰鬥方式並無法解決衝突的根本原因。

paragraphs.en: Because the situation is so complex, the election results are very hard to predict. To win the presidency, a candidate usually needs more than 50% of the total votes. If no one reaches this number, which is common in many countries but rare in Colombia, the two candidates with the most votes will face each other in a final runoff election in June. This second round would force the country to make a final choice between the two different visions for peace and security.
paragraphs.zh: 由於局勢極其複雜，選舉結果難以預測。候選人通常需要獲得超過總票數的百分之五十才能贏得總統大位。若無人達到此門檻——這在許多國家很常見，但在哥倫比亞卻很罕見——得票數最高的兩位候選人將在六月的最終決選中一決勝負。這第二輪投票將迫使國家在兩種截然不同的和平與安全願景之間做出最終抉擇。

paragraphs.en: As the votes are counted, the world is watching closely. The outcome will not just affect the daily lives of Colombians, but it will also set the tone for how the country interacts with its neighbors and the United States. Whether the next leader chooses to continue the path of negotiation or turns toward a more aggressive military strategy, the decision will have long-lasting effects on the stability of the nation. For now, the people of Colombia must wait to see which vision for their country will win the support of the majority.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著選票開出，全世界都在密切關注。選舉結果不僅會影響哥倫比亞人的日常生活，也將為該國與鄰國及美國的互動定下基調。無論下一任領導人選擇延續談判路線，還是轉向更激進的軍事策略，這項決定都將對國家的穩定產生深遠影響。目前，哥倫比亞人民只能等待，看看哪一種國家願景能贏得多數人的支持。

keyTerms.definitionEn: Participating in an election as a candidate.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「跑步」，這裡指參與競選。文中：Running against Cepeda are two candidates, Abelardo de la Espriella and Paloma Valencia, who promise to use a much stronger hand against armed groups.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A firm position or opinion on a particular issue.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（站立），這裡作名詞，指對議題的立場。文中：Maria Eugenia, a 57-year-old seamstress, told The Guardian that she wants the government to take a very strong stand against criminal groups.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To establish the general mood or character of something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 字面意思為「設定音調」，實際用於比喻決定某事物的發展方向或氛圍。文中：The outcome will not just affect the daily lives of Colombians, but it will also set the tone for how the country interacts with its neighbors and the United States.

sources.name: The Guardian World
sources.title: Colombia goes to polls in election pitting outgoing leader’s ally against pro-Trump candidates

quiz.question: What is the primary criticism critics have leveled against President Petro's 'total peace' plan?

options.A: It has caused the minimum wage to decrease.
options.B: It has allowed criminal groups to regroup during the ceasefire.
options.C: It focuses too much on military force rather than negotiation.
options.D: It has failed to gain support from international allies like the United States.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，批評者認為該計畫並未奏效，因為犯罪集團利用停火協議（ceasefire）的空窗期來重組並繼續非法活動。選項 A 與文意相反，文章提到最低工資是正面改變；選項 C 錯誤，因為該計畫的核心是談判而非軍事；選項 D 文中並未提及該計畫與美國支持度的直接關聯。

quiz.question: Based on the text, what can be inferred about the political views of Abelardo de la Espriella and Paloma Valencia?

options.A: They believe that the current government's approach to security is too aggressive.
options.B: They prioritize human rights over the immediate reduction of criminal activity.
options.C: They favor a more confrontational and security-heavy approach to governance.
options.D: They are likely to continue the 'total peace' plan if they are elected.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章第三段與第四段指出，這兩位候選人承諾對武裝團體採取更強硬的手段，並表達對川普的支持，認為哥倫比亞應採取更具侵略性的安全策略。選項 A 錯誤，他們認為政府太軟弱；選項 B 錯誤，文中暗示他們的方法可能導致人權問題；選項 D 錯誤，他們是現任政府政策的反對者。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the phrase 'set the tone' mean in the context of Colombia's future international relations?

options.A: To establish the general character or atmosphere of future interactions.
options.B: To play a musical instrument to signal the start of a diplomatic meeting.
options.C: To force neighboring countries to change their own laws.
options.D: To ignore the opinions of other nations while making decisions.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在此語境下，"set the tone" 指的是確立某種氛圍或方針，影響未來與鄰國及美國互動的基調。選項 B 是字面上的錯誤解讀；選項 C 過度誇大，文中並未提到強迫他國修法；選項 D 與文意不符，因為文章強調該選舉結果會影響與他國的關係，而非忽視他國。

quiz.question: Which statement best summarizes the central theme of the article?

options.A: The presidential election in Colombia is primarily focused on economic reform and wage increases.
options.B: Colombia's election highlights a deep national divide over how to achieve security and peace.
options.C: The international community is actively interfering in the Colombian election process.
options.D: Most voters in Colombia are in agreement that military force is the only way to end violence.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。整篇文章圍繞著哥倫比亞總統大選，探討了支持「總體和平」談判路線與支持「強硬軍事」路線之間的深刻分歧。選項 A 僅提到經濟改革的一小部分，非核心議題；選項 C 文中僅提到國際社會在「關注」而非「干預」；選項 D 錯誤，因為文中明確指出選民對此有嚴重分歧，並非達成共識。
